mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.25% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 315, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained tightness compresses equity valuations and increases discount rates, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for duration risk.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 319.775 indicate persistent tightening regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of 20-day periods when trigger condition is active, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.3% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. Investors flee to safety, increasing demand for long-duration Treasury bonds and driving yields lower as equity risk premiums compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.1% is near trigger threshold
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of 20-day periods following UNRATE > 4.3%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.3 million annualized units, indicating weakening aggregate demand. This demand shock reduces inflation expectations and causes the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as growth concerns dominate.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 723853M and HOUST at 1366K suggest demand near inflection point
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of 60-day periods when both conditions are met, or correlation between RSAFS-HOUST joint signal and yield direction is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 104.0 (index level) while payroll employment (PAYEMS) expands by more than 200K per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion. This productivity surge reduces inflation pressures and allows the Fed to pause tightening, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as rate-cut expectations increase.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.511 and PAYEMS at 159218K show moderate supply conditions
regime_hint: productivity-driven expansion with labor market strength
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of 20-day periods when trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 50 basis points over a 3-month window, signaling monetary easing. This policy pivot reduces discount rates on future cash flows and increases equity valuations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors rotate from bonds to equities and inflation expectations stabilize.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% provides room for easing
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following tightening peak
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of 60-day periods following a 50bps rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) contract month-over-month, indicating a demand-driven recession. Flight-to-safety flows drive 10-year Treasury yields lower as investors price in lower future short rates and reduced inflation.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND RSAFS MoM contraction
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 at 23536.293B (5 months stale) requires monitoring for deceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of 60-day periods when both recession signals are present, or yield-growth correlation becomes positive (contradicting flight-to-safety mechanism).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K per month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains stable or rises, signaling labor market cooling. This softening reduces wage pressure and inflation expectations, allowing the Fed to cut rates, which causes the 10-year Treasury yield to fall.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159218K and UNRATE at 4.1% show labor market near inflection
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of 20-day periods when both conditions are met, or labor market cooling does not precede Fed rate cuts by 2-3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.2%, indicating supply-side contraction and labor market weakness. This stagflationary signal causes the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk and economic uncertainty.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.511 and UNRATE at 4.1% near stagflation threshold
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of 5-day periods when both conditions are triggered, or stagflation premium (yield rise despite growth concerns) fails to materialize in more than 60% of instances.
